auth.cantReachBody: We couldn’t verify the dashboard state. Check that the orchestrator is running, then try again.

login.createSub: Create the admin password to get started.
login.noRecovery: This is the only admin account. There’s no email reset — store this password somewhere safe.
login.errMin: Password must be at least 8 characters.

setup.title: Set up NurProxy
setup.intro: Two quick steps. New to this? <0>Read the guide</0>.
setup.providerSub: Paste an API token and we’ll detect the domains it can manage.
setup.tokenHint: Needs <0>Zone → Read</0> and <1>DNS → Edit</1>. Stored encrypted at rest.
setup.whereBody: In Cloudflare, open <0>My Profile → API Tokens</0>, click <1>Create Token</1>, and use the <2>“Edit zone DNS”</2> template. You can scope it to only the zones you want, or all zones in your account. <3>Full walkthrough →</3>
setup.noZones: Token is valid, but no zones were found. Make sure it has Zone → Read for the domains you want.
setup.manageHint: You can manage domains under each zone afterwards.
setup.agentSub: Run the NurProxy agent on the edge server that will serve your traffic.
setup.orchestratorHint: This is how the agent reaches this dashboard. It must be reachable from the edge server — often a LAN IP, VPN address, or public hostname, not the URL in your browser’s address bar.
setup.fqdnHint: The public hostname for this edge server. The agent creates an A record for it at your DNS provider pointing to the server’s IP, and every subdomain you add for this agent becomes a CNAME to it. Change it only if you want a different anchor hostname than the server’s own FQDN.
setup.runThis: Run this on your edge server
setup.agentAdoptedBody: It’s connected and ready to serve domains.
setup.waiting: Waiting for the agent to connect…
setup.troubleReach: Make sure {{url}} is reachable from the edge server (try curl it there).
setup.troubleFirewall: Check the server’s firewall isn’t blocking outbound HTTPS.
setup.troubleLogs: Look at the agent logs for connection errors.
setup.noZonesYet: No zones yet — add a provider first.
setup.addLater: You can add more providers and agents anytime from Settings and Agents.

overview.title: Overview
overview.noAgentsBody: Install the NurProxy agent on an edge server. It registers itself and shows up here for approval.

agents.title: Agents
agents.subtitle: Edge servers running NurProxy. Select one to inspect it.
agents.noneBody: Install the NurProxy agent on a server and it will appear here for approval. See Setup for the install command.
agents.select: Select an agent to see details.
agents.awaitingBody: Approve this agent to give it zones and a DNS mode, or reject it if you don’t recognize it.
agents.deleteConfirm: Delete “{{name}}”? This removes all of its servers and domains too.
agents.anchorFqdnHelp: The DNS name that points at this server. It must live inside one of the agent’s zones — every domain you add becomes a CNAME to it.
agents.anchorFqdnWarn: This hostname isn’t inside any selected zone. DNS for it can’t be managed until you pick the matching zone or change the hostname.
agents.noProxyDetected: No supported reverse proxy detected on this host.
agents.bindConflictBody: Another process is holding the ports the built-in proxy needs. Switch this agent to an existing-proxy setup, or free the port.
agents.bindConflictChoiceFree: Stop the existing proxy and let the built-in proxy take the ports.
agents.bindConflictChoiceSwitch: Switch this agent to manage the existing proxy instead of running the built-in one —

existing.title: Set up Existing proxy
existing.intro: Manage a proxy already installed on this host instead of the built-in one. Confirm the detected paths below — you won’t usually need to change them.
existing.detectedBody: {{name}} was detected — the fields below are pre-filled from it. Confirm or adjust.
existing.noDetection: Nothing was detected for this proxy type. The fields are pre-filled with the OS defaults — confirm they match this host before applying.
existing.proxyKindHint: Which proxy this agent should manage. Pre-selected from detection.
existing.logPathsHint: One path per line. Surfaced in the dashboard for on-demand tailing.
existing.applyHint: Proxy mode lives in the agent’s own config. Add these keys to the agent’s config file (or pass them as flags) on the host, then restart the agent.
existing.permsNote: The agent must be able to write the config dir and run the reload/test commands — grant a group-owned dir plus a narrowly-scoped sudoers entry for exactly those commands, not blanket sudo.

servers.title: Servers
servers.subtitle: Backend addresses your agents proxy to. Each belongs to one agent.
servers.approveFirstBody: Servers live behind an agent. Connect and approve an agent, then register the servers it proxies to.
servers.noneYetBody: Register the first backend an agent should forward traffic to — e.g. a web app on your LAN.
servers.onAgentNone: No servers on this agent yet.
servers.addModalSub: A backend address one of your agents forwards traffic to.
servers.agentHint: The agent that will reach this server.
servers.addressHint: An IP or hostname reachable from this agent — i.e. how the agent reaches the server (LAN IP, container name, VPN address).
servers.removeConfirm: Remove this server? Domains pointing at it will be affected until you repoint them.

domains.title: Domains
domains.subtitle: Subdomains proxied to your servers.
domains.noneYetBody: Create your first domain to start proxying traffic to a server.
domains.noMatchesBody: No domains match the current filters.
domains.manualConfigBody: Editing the raw Caddy JSON overrides the automatic config for this domain. Reset to automatic anytime.
domains.bulkMsg_one: Delete {{count}} domain? Its DNS record and proxy config will be removed.
domains.bulkMsg_other: Delete {{count}} domains? Their DNS records and proxy configs will be removed.

settings.title: Settings
settings.appearanceSub: Pick the interface that suits you. Switch light/dark from the toggle in the navigation.
settings.reconcilerSub: How often NurProxy syncs DNS records and proxy configs.
settings.adminApiKeySub: Use this as a <0>Bearer</0> token for programmatic access and the MCP server.
settings.copyNow: Copy this now — it won’t be shown again
settings.tokenHint: Needs <0>Zone → Read</0> and <1>DNS → Edit</1>. Stored encrypted at rest.
settings.providerCallout: Create a token in <0>Cloudflare → API Tokens</0> using the “Edit zone DNS” template. <1>Walkthrough →</1>
settings.tokenNoZones: Token is valid, but no zones were found. Check Zone → Read permission.
settings.pwTooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters.
settings.deleteProviderMsg: Delete this provider? All zones and domains using it will lose DNS management.
settings.removeZoneMsg: Remove this zone? Domains using it will lose DNS management.

classic.name: Classic
classic.description: A compact top-nav dashboard with a health panel, cards, and tables.

workbench.name: Workbench
workbench.description: A live topology map of your infrastructure with a sidebar and click-to-inspect panels.

wallboard.name: Wallboard
wallboard.description: A read-only status grid for a spare monitor. Big, glanceable, auto-refreshing.

spreadsheet.name: Spreadsheet
spreadsheet.description: A dense, table-first view with multi-select and bulk actions for power users.

topology.title: Topology
topology.subtitle: Your edge agents, upstreams, and domains. Click to inspect · right-click for actions.
topology.nothingBody: Connect an agent and add a domain — they’ll appear here as a live graph of your setup.
topology.deleteAgentMsg: Delete “{{name}}”? Removes its servers and domains too.
topology.removeServerMsg: Remove “{{name}}”? Domains using it are affected.
topology.inspectorInternet: Inbound requests resolve via DNS to an edge agent.

help.title: Docs & help
help.subtitle: Everything about NurProxy — available offline, right here.

config.title: Config
config.subtitle: The central versioned store of proxy config NurProxy manages on your hosts. Diff, roll back, and review on-disk drift before it overwrites your changes.
config.noneBody: Once an agent applies a domain or adopts an existing config, its versioned artifacts appear here.
config.select: Select an artifact to view its content, history, and drift.
config.driftedBody: The content on the host no longer matches the accepted state. Review the diff, then Accept the on-disk content as the new live version or Reject to restore the accepted state.
config.driftChecksumOnly: The host reported a different checksum but hasn't sent its on-disk content yet (it arrives on the next apply). Accept to take the host's current state, or Reject to restore the accepted version now.
config.bulkRowNote: Accepted state shown; the host reported a checksum mismatch.
config.bulkBannerBody: Several configs changed on-disk at once (for example after an OS package update). Review them together and accept or reject all.
config.bulkModalWarn: Accept all promotes every host's on-disk content to the live state. Reject all restores the accepted state to every host.
config.maskFailed: Failed to parse the structured view.
config.maskAdvisory: This config can't be fully represented as a form yet, so the structured view is read-only. The raw text is authoritative.
config.maskBackendRawOnly: The structured view isn't available for this backend yet. Edit the raw config.
config.saved: Saved. The change will be applied on the host.
config.editFlipsManual: Editing the raw config will mark this artifact as Manual — it can no longer be re-rendered from the model without resetting.
config.resetToModelConfirm: Re-render this artifact from its domain model and discard the manual edits?
config.resetDone: Reset to the model. The agent will re-render on the next apply.
